# Trace to Reader

## Reader paragraph A-READER-001

Uses C1 and K1. The reader page starts by making the extracted body the primary material. This prevents the AI page from becoming the book substitute.

## Reader paragraph A-READER-002

Uses C2 and K2. The central framing is phrased as an interpretation: the book challenges painful productivity as a default diagnosis.

## Reader paragraph A-READER-003

Uses C3 and K5. The hospital opening is treated as diagnostic evidence, not as a general claim that all pressure is bad.

## Reader paragraph A-READER-004

Uses C4 and K3. Play, control, and connection are promoted as energy-system ideas, while lightweight happiness advice is downgraded.

## Reader paragraph A-READER-005

Uses C5 and K4. The reader page promotes action design and keeps the emphasis on clarity, first steps, and fear naming.

## Reader paragraph A-READER-006

Uses C6 and K6. Rest and alignment are framed as maintenance and calibration, not as universal relaxation advice.

## Reader paragraph A-READER-007

Uses C7 and K7. The page rejects happy-productivity absolutism and keeps the book's claims within the selected source.
